page: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv7 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This configuration features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache per core. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This dense memory allocation positions the instance for workloads requiring substantial RAM relative to compute capacity. The instance does not include local storage, but it is premium storage capable, allowing users to attach external persistent disks. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this virtual machine.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also achieves top-tier status. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and passmark CPU scores fall within the average tier (middle 50%). Memory bandwidth tests, including cached small block reads and uncached large block reads/writes, consistently register average performance. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speed (351.12 tokens/sec) and strong prompt processing speed (2460.02 tokens/sec) using a small 135M model, though performance drops to average levels when scaling to a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F8ams_v7 offers high memory density per vCPU, which is advantageous for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires factoring in the cost of external storage. The instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, redis caching, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the memory-intensive Famsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz with 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but includes no bundled local storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, contrasted with average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The processor supports AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration. This instance is suited for memory-bound workloads, database operations, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-intensive VM with 64 GB RAM, an AMD EPYC processor, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring high memory density, dedicated single-thread performance, and advanced cryptographic acceleration for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per vCPU)
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Premium storage capable with no bundled local storage
Supports AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and SHA_NI instruction sets
Suited for database operations, caching, and small-scale LLM inference
